expected: creates a planned task
copies plan.md into ~/.kanban/<repo>/<uuid>/plan.md
requires at least one role entry with a non-empty brief
treats plan.md as an index document
copies linked plan-*.md files from the same directory
assigns developer seats whose briefs correspond to the sub-plans

cases.input: /kanban --role developer from a worktree with an idle preassigned developer seat
expected: offers to claim the preassigned seat
keeps the stable key and records cwd
reads plan.md and the matching plan-*.md before starting work

cases.input: reviewer context with a developer entry in waiting_review
expected: reads the dev report file
checks the git diff before deciding
writes review-<dev>-<NN>.md
updates developer status through agent-write.ts

cases.input: automatic kanban trigger for a task with status=draft
expected: does not enter role work mode
tells the user to promote the task to planned first
does not mutate kanban state

cases.input: /kanban --role test from a legacy tester entry
expected: accepts test as a legacy alias
writes canonical role as tester
does not persist task.test

cases.input: query.ts from reviewer, tester, and integrator cwd contexts
expected: JSON includes currentEntry with role, key, status, and brief
JSON includes eligibleReviewTargets for waiting_review developers
JSON includes testerBlockedBy for developers not yet review_approved
JSON includes integratorBlockedBy for entries not done or blocked
JSON preserves idleStations for role seat claiming
recommendedNextAction is a short hint

cases.input: issue.ts open, done, and closed for a tester entry
expected: open writes issue file and moves owner developer to follow_issue
open moves tester to waiting and records fail
done requires owner developer to be review_approved
done archives the issue and records tester pass
closed archives the issue without requiring a developer fix

cases.input: developer submits waiting_review while an owner open issue exists
expected: agent-write rejects a dev report without related_issue
the error names the required issue file
a dev report that references related_issue can proceed
